message:
fuzz: add structure-aware dictionaries + sanitizer doc (br-frankenpandas-gztr)

Closes br-frankenpandas-gztr: apply /testing-fuzzing Rule 4 (structure-aware > random bytes 10:1 on structured formats) + Rule 5 (ASan without UBSan is half a tool).

New libFuzzer dictionaries under fuzz/dictionaries/ for csv, json, excel, parquet, arrow_ipc formats. fuzz/README.md documents the dictionary catalog, the weekly minimization job (br-i9rj), and the ASan+UBSan sanitizer recipe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-gsq6","title":"fp-index Index.asof (nearest-preceding lookup) parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:49:22.296852910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:58:10.959292697Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:58:10.957556580Z","close_reason":"Implemented Index::asof in commit b17e94a, 2 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-gwa2","title":"fp-frame: DataFrame.mode() preserves Int64 when padded with nulls (dtype parity)","description":"Observed failure: fp-conformance tests::packet_filter_runs_dataframe_rank_mode_packet fails on FP-P2D-057/dataframe_mode_ties_strict: 'dataframe column b mismatch at idx=0: actual=Float64(3.0), expected=Int64(3)'.

Fixture: columns a=[1,1,2,2] (2 tied modes) and b=[3,3,3,4] (1 mode). max_modes=2, so column b gets padded with Null(NaN) to match. Expected (per pandas oracle): column b dtype stays Int64, null cell remains Null(NaN) with int64 kind. Actual: fp-frame widens to Float64.

Root: crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:411 fn mode_output_dtype returns DType::Float64 when saw_missing || saw_int is true. The branch 'saw_float || saw_missing' conflates 'original dtype included floats' with 'padding added a null' — the latter shouldn't drive dtype widening because the original column was Int64 and pandas keeps Int64 with a null.

Fix: separate the padding-induced saw_missing from genuine float widening. If we saw only Int64 values plus nulls (from padding), return Some(DType::Int64); only widen to Float64 when genuine Float64 values are present or when Bool+Int mix. Unit test: verify mode_output_dtype([Int64, Null]) -> Int64 and column b output preserves Int64 kind.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:35.315784398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:57:10.497455446Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:57:10.495528834Z","close_reason":"shipped b544717: mode_axis1 pre-promotes Int->Float when any selected source column is Float64. Companion mode_output_dtype dtype-preservation fix and 3 new tests co-landed in 94fcdca. Verified packet_filter_runs_dataframe_rank_mode_packet now passes; 9/9 df_mode tests green; fp-frame clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-gwga","title":"Add Series.pct_change limit parameter parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-20T18:14:15.410174104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:16:10.748815695Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:16:10.745626675Z","close_reason":"Implemented pct_change_with_fill supporting ffill/bfill/pad/backfill + limit with 5 unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankenpandas-gztr","title":"[MEDIUM] fuzz: dictionaries missing for structured formats + sanitizer flags not configured","description":"Applying /testing-fuzzing skill's checklist and DICTIONARIES.md reference.

**Observed state:** fuzz/Cargo.toml has 21 [[bin]] blocks, no sanitizer mentions. No fuzz/dictionaries/ dir. No README documenting which sanitizer each target requires or how to run with ASan+UBSan.

**Missing skill requirements:**
- Rule 5: "ASan without UBSan is half a tool. Always enable both." cargo-fuzz on stable Rust defaults to ASan-only. UBSan needs RUSTFLAGS='-Zsanitizer=address,undefined' under nightly or a `.cargo/config.toml` override.
- Rule 6: "Run separate sanitizer campaigns with shared corpora." — no documented MSan campaign for the fp-runtime asupersync path (which has unsafe via the dependency graph).
- Rule 4: "Structure-aware beats random bytes 10:1 on structured formats." fp-io parses CSV, JSON, JSONL, Excel, Parquet, Feather, Arrow IPC — all structured. The Arbitrary-derive macros in fp-conformance's fuzz_*_frame_from_bytes helpers do partial structure-awareness, but format-specific dictionaries (keywords, delimiters, magic bytes) are absent.

**Fix shape:**
1. fuzz/dictionaries/csv.dict   — quoted-newline, escaped-quote, BOM, comma/pipe/tab delimiters, RFC 4180 edge strings.
2. fuzz/dictionaries/json.dict  — {}, [], "\\u", NaN, Infinity, escape sequences, surrogate pairs.
3. fuzz/dictionaries/excel.dict — XLSX magic bytes PK\x03\x04, zip64 markers, shared-strings-table tokens.
4. fuzz/dictionaries/parquet.dict — PAR1 magic, Thrift type tokens, page-header bytes.
5. fuzz/dictionaries/arrow_ipc.dict — ARROW1, stream end marker, schema flatbuffer tokens.
6. fuzz/README.md documenting: per-target dictionary, required sanitizer, recommended runtime, how to reproduce a crash.
7. .cargo/config.toml or fuzz/.cargo/config.toml setting RUSTFLAGS='-Zsanitizer=address,undefined' for the `fuzz` profile (nightly-only; behind a feature flag or comment).

Skill reference: DICTIONARIES.md in testing-fuzzing/references.

Priority MEDIUM — no known bug; this is a force multiplier for future fuzz campaigns (expected 5-50x speedup on the JSON/CSV/Excel surfaces per the skill's stated impact).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:45:58.955338163Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:45:58.955338163Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dictionaries","fuzz","review","sanitizer"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-gztr","title":"[MEDIUM] fuzz: dictionaries missing for structured formats + sanitizer flags not configured","description":"Applying /testing-fuzzing skill's checklist and DICTIONARIES.md reference.

**Observed state:** fuzz/Cargo.toml has 21 [[bin]] blocks, no sanitizer mentions. No fuzz/dictionaries/ dir. No README documenting which sanitizer each target requires or how to run with ASan+UBSan.

**Missing skill requirements:**
- Rule 5: "ASan without UBSan is half a tool. Always enable both." cargo-fuzz on stable Rust defaults to ASan-only. UBSan needs RUSTFLAGS='-Zsanitizer=address,undefined' under nightly or a `.cargo/config.toml` override.
- Rule 6: "Run separate sanitizer campaigns with shared corpora." — no documented MSan campaign for the fp-runtime asupersync path (which has unsafe via the dependency graph).
- Rule 4: "Structure-aware beats random bytes 10:1 on structured formats." fp-io parses CSV, JSON, JSONL, Excel, Parquet, Feather, Arrow IPC — all structured. The Arbitrary-derive macros in fp-conformance's fuzz_*_frame_from_bytes helpers do partial structure-awareness, but format-specific dictionaries (keywords, delimiters, magic bytes) are absent.

**Fix shape:**
1. fuzz/dictionaries/csv.dict   — quoted-newline, escaped-quote, BOM, comma/pipe/tab delimiters, RFC 4180 edge strings.
2. fuzz/dictionaries/json.dict  — {}, [], "\\u", NaN, Infinity, escape sequences, surrogate pairs.
3. fuzz/dictionaries/excel.dict — XLSX magic bytes PK\x03\x04, zip64 markers, shared-strings-table tokens.
4. fuzz/dictionaries/parquet.dict — PAR1 magic, Thrift type tokens, page-header bytes.
5. fuzz/dictionaries/arrow_ipc.dict — ARROW1, stream end marker, schema flatbuffer tokens.
6. fuzz/README.md documenting: per-target dictionary, required sanitizer, recommended runtime, how to reproduce a crash.
7. .cargo/config.toml or fuzz/.cargo/config.toml setting RUSTFLAGS='-Zsanitizer=address,undefined' for the `fuzz` profile (nightly-only; behind a feature flag or comment).

Skill reference: DICTIONARIES.md in testing-fuzzing/references.

 {"id":"frankenpandas-no1t","title":"pandas: fp-frame DataFrameGroupBy.transform callable-closure parity","description":"py signature:
df.groupby('k').transform(lambda s: (s - s.mean()) / s.std()) -> DataFrame

Current DataFrameGroupBy.transform only takes func_name: &str (calls apply_agg_func then broadcasts). Pandas supports passing a callable that receives a Series (per-group, per-column) and returns a same-length Series. Add transform_fn<F>(func: F) where F: Fn(&Series) -> Result<Series, FrameError>, iterating each value column and each group, invoking the closure, and reassembling in original row order.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:51:02.737433381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:16:10.671278365Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:16:10.669428772Z","close_reason":"Implemented callable groupby transform parity in commit 7f8d8f6","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankenpandas-npki","title":"[MEDIUM] hygiene: no cargo-machete / cargo-udeps in CI — unused deps inflate compile time + supply-chain surface","description":"Unused-dependency hygiene audit.

**Observation.** grep for `machete|udeps|unused-dep` in .github/workflows/ci.yml → zero matches. No `cargo machete` or `cargo +nightly udeps` step anywhere.

Workspace has substantial deps (arrow 58, parquet 58, calamine 0.34, rusqlite 0.39, chrono 0.4, chrono-tz 0.10, bumpalo 3.20, bytes 1.11, rust_xlsxwriter 0.94, serde 1.0.228, serde_json 1.0.149, regex 1.12, thiserror 2.0.18, unicode-casefold 0.2, raptorq 2.0, sha2 0.11, serde_yaml 0.9.34-deprecated, proptest 1.11, tempfile 3.27) and recently gained more through the SQL backend epic.

**Why this matters today.**
- `cargo build` compiles every declared dep, not just the used ones. Unused deps inflate compile time + binary size.
- Dependency audit (br-36qc cargo-audit) surfaces CVEs in ALL deps — including unused ones. A vuln in an unused dep is a false alarm that distracts from real findings.
- Supply-chain attack surface: every dep is a potential vector. Fewer deps = smaller surface.
- The library-updater sweeps (UPGRADE_LOG.md) touch every dep, even unused ones. Cleanup would narrow future sweep scope.

**Concrete risk example.** `serde_yaml = "0.9.34+deprecated"` sits in fp-conformance for YAML parity-gate parsing. If conformance gates drop YAML support, serde_yaml becomes an unused-but-still-compiled dep. We currently have no signal that would surface this.

**Severity.** MEDIUM — compound cost is real but gradual. No shipped-code impact today. Force-multiplier for cleaner supply-chain posture + faster compiles. Honest calibration: not HIGH.

**Fix shape:**

1. CI job (either in `checks` or the post-ffhs split):
   ```yaml
   - name: Install cargo-machete
     run: cargo install cargo-machete --locked

   - name: Check unused deps
     run: cargo machete
   ```

2. Alternative / complementary: `cargo +nightly udeps` — catches unused deps more thoroughly but requires nightly (we're already on nightly per br-1d9y).

3. Allowlist for legitimate declare-but-don't-directly-use cases:
   `.cargo-machete.toml`:
   ```toml
   [package]
   ignored = []  # populate if any false positives surface
   ```

4. Pre-commit hook (optional): run `cargo machete` on affected Cargo.toml only when those files change in staged diff.

5. One-time cleanup pass after the bead lands: remove any deps cargo-machete flags. Expect ~0-3 actual removals.

Blast radius: 1 CI step (~8 lines), 1 optional allowlist file (~5 lines), zero source changes on the happy path, minor Cargo.toml trimming if real unused deps surface.

Prerequisites:
- br-ffhs (CI job splitting) — natural new job ("hygiene").
- br-1d9y (nightly pin) — needed for `cargo udeps` alternative.

**Related:**
- br-36qc (cargo-audit) — same CI family; both are dependency hygiene. Bundle in a single "security + hygiene" job.
- br-hg60 (secret scan) — same family.
- UPGRADE_LOG.md (lib-updater workflow) — this bead's output narrows future sweep scope.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:43:18.198543899Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:43:18.198543899Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","dependencies","hygiene","review"]}

Workspace has substantial deps (arrow 58, parquet 58, calamine 0.34, rusqlite 0.39, chrono 0.4, chrono-tz 0.10, bumpalo 3.20, bytes 1.11, rust_xlsxwriter 0.94, serde 1.0.228, serde_json 1.0.149, regex 1.12, thiserror 2.0.18, unicode-casefold 0.2, raptorq 2.0, sha2 0.11, serde_yaml 0.9.34-deprecated, proptest 1.11, tempfile 3.27) and recently gained more through the SQL backend epic.

**Why this matters today.**
- `cargo build` compiles every declared dep, not just the used ones. Unused deps inflate compile time + binary size.
- Dependency audit (br-36qc cargo-audit) surfaces CVEs in ALL deps — including unused ones. A vuln in an unused dep is a false alarm that distracts from real findings.
- Supply-chain attack surface: every dep is a potential vector. Fewer deps = smaller surface.
- The library-updater sweeps (UPGRADE_LOG.md) touch every dep, even unused ones. Cleanup would narrow future sweep scope.

**Concrete risk example.** `serde_yaml = "0.9.34+deprecated"` sits in fp-conformance for YAML parity-gate parsing. If conformance gates drop YAML support, serde_yaml becomes an unused-but-still-compiled dep. We currently have no signal that would surface this.

**Severity.** MEDIUM — compound cost is real but gradual. No shipped-code impact today. Force-multiplier for cleaner supply-chain posture + faster compiles. Honest calibration: not HIGH.

**Fix shape:**

1. CI job (either in `checks` or the post-ffhs split):

   ```yaml
   - name: Install cargo-machete
     run: cargo install cargo-machete --locked

   - name: Check unused deps
     run: cargo machete
   ```

2. Alternative / complementary: `cargo +nightly udeps` — catches unused deps more thoroughly but requires nightly (we're already on nightly per br-1d9y).

3. Allowlist for legitimate declare-but-don't-directly-use cases:
   `.cargo-machete.toml`:
   ```toml
   [package]
   ignored = []  # populate if any false positives surface
   ```

4. Pre-commit hook (optional): run `cargo machete` on affected Cargo.toml only when those files change in staged diff.

5. One-time cleanup pass after the bead lands: remove any deps cargo-machete flags. Expect ~0-3 actual removals.

Blast radius: 1 CI step (~8 lines), 1 optional allowlist file (~5 lines), zero source changes on the happy path, minor Cargo.toml trimming if real unused deps surface.

Prerequisites:
- br-ffhs (CI job splitting) — natural new job ("hygiene").
- br-1d9y (nightly pin) — needed for `cargo udeps` alternative.

**Related:**

- br-hg60 (secret scan) — same family.
- UPGRADE_LOG.md (lib-updater workflow) — this bead's output narrows future sweep scope.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:43:18.198543899Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:34:30.733874951Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:34:30.731186655Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","dependencies","hygiene","review"]}

-{"id":"frankenpandas-xgsf","title":"[MEDIUM] style: no .editorconfig / .gitattributes — non-Rust style drifts silently across swarm + Windows line-ending risk","description":"Cross-language style hygiene audit.

**Observation.**
- `ls .editorconfig` → No such file.
- `ls .gitattributes` → No such file.

**Context.** The repository contains files in multiple languages:
- Rust (.rs, .toml)
- Python (oracle/pandas_oracle.py, generate_asof.py, test_asof.py, pre-commit hooks)
- Shell scripts (scripts/*.sh)
- YAML (.github/workflows/*.yml)
- Markdown (README.md, AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG.md, DISCREPANCIES.md, COVERAGE.md, etc.)
- JSON fixture files (~1249)
- JSONL bead store (.beads/issues.jsonl)

Without `.editorconfig`, every editor applies its own defaults:
- Indent size: some editors 2-space, some 4-space for YAML/MD.
- Trailing whitespace: silently preserved by some editors, stripped by others.
- Newline at EOF: some editors force it, some don't.
- Line endings: Windows contributors (if/when br-0l5r matrix lands) may silently introduce CRLF in text files.

**Consequence.** In a multi-agent swarm committing across Rust + Python + YAML + Markdown, style drift compounds. Today's only style enforcement is `cargo fmt --check` for Rust (+ the proposed rustfmt.toml in br-thty). Non-Rust files drift silently. Diffs show phantom whitespace / line-ending changes mixed in with real content changes, making review harder.

**Severity.** MEDIUM (not HIGH) — drift is gradual, review friction is real but not blocking. The swarm velocity amplifies it, but no shipped user experience is degraded. Calibrating honestly per my own pass-10 s5vn lesson.

**Fix shape:**

1. `.editorconfig` at repo root:
   ```
   root = true

   [*]
   charset = utf-8
   end_of_line = lf
   indent_style = space
   indent_size = 4
   insert_final_newline = true
   trim_trailing_whitespace = true

   [*.{yml,yaml,md,json,toml}]
   indent_size = 2

   [*.rs]
   indent_size = 4          # matches rustfmt

   [Makefile]
   indent_style = tab

   [*.py]
   indent_size = 4          # matches PEP 8
   ```

2. `.gitattributes` at repo root:
   ```
   # Auto-detect text files and normalize line endings to LF on commit.
   * text=auto eol=lf

   # Explicit per-file overrides where needed:
   *.png binary
   *.webp binary
   *.xlsx binary
   *.xls binary
   *.parquet binary
   *.feather binary

   # Large files that should use git-lfs eventually (tracked for future):
   # *.jsonl diff=jsonl   (custom diff driver for bead store)
   ```

3. Optional: `[*.{jsonl}] end_of_line = lf insert_final_newline = false` — bead JSONL expects no-trailing-newline per append-only invariant.

Blast radius: 2 new tiny files at repo root (~20-30 lines total). Zero source changes. Zero test changes.

Prerequisites: None. Coordinate with br-thty (rustfmt.toml) + br-0l5r (cross-platform CI, where line-ending drift would first surface) for maximum impact.

**Related:**
- br-thty (rustfmt.toml) — companion style-enforcement for Rust.
- br-0l5r (cross-platform CI) — Windows matrix cell would hit CRLF issues without this.
- br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — one-liner reference: "Your editor should auto-apply .editorconfig."","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:42:38.537873244Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:42:38.537873244Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-platform","editorconfig","review","style"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-xgsf","title":"[MEDIUM] style: no .editorconfig / .gitattributes — non-Rust style drifts silently across swarm + Windows line-ending risk","description":"Cross-language style hygiene audit.

**Observation.**
- `ls .editorconfig` → No such file.
- `ls .gitattributes` → No such file.

**Context.** The repository contains files in multiple languages:
- Rust (.rs, .toml)
- Python (oracle/pandas_oracle.py, generate_asof.py, test_asof.py, pre-commit hooks)
- Shell scripts (scripts/*.sh)
- YAML (.github/workflows/*.yml)
- Markdown (README.md, AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG.md, DISCREPANCIES.md, COVERAGE.md, etc.)
- JSON fixture files (~1249)
- JSONL bead store (.beads/issues.jsonl)

Without `.editorconfig`, every editor applies its own defaults:
- Indent size: some editors 2-space, some 4-space for YAML/MD.
- Trailing whitespace: silently preserved by some editors, stripped by others.
- Newline at EOF: some editors force it, some don't.
- Line endings: Windows contributors (if/when br-0l5r matrix lands) may silently introduce CRLF in text files.

**Consequence.** In a multi-agent swarm committing across Rust + Python + YAML + Markdown, style drift compounds. Today's only style enforcement is `cargo fmt --check` for Rust (+ the proposed rustfmt.toml in br-thty). Non-Rust files drift silently. Diffs show phantom whitespace / line-ending changes mixed in with real content changes, making review harder.

**Severity.** MEDIUM (not HIGH) — drift is gradual, review friction is real but not blocking. The swarm velocity amplifies it, but no shipped user experience is degraded. Calibrating honestly per my own pass-10 s5vn lesson.

**Fix shape:**

1. `.editorconfig` at repo root:
   ```
   root = true

   [*]
   charset = utf-8
   end_of_line = lf
   indent_style = space
   indent_size = 4
   insert_final_newline = true
   trim_trailing_whitespace = true

   [*.{yml,yaml,md,json,toml}]
   indent_size = 2

   [*.rs]
   indent_size = 4          # matches rustfmt

   [Makefile]
   indent_style = tab

   [*.py]
   indent_size = 4          # matches PEP 8
   ```

2. `.gitattributes` at repo root:
   ```
   # Auto-detect text files and normalize line endings to LF on commit.
   * text=auto eol=lf

   # Explicit per-file overrides where needed:
   *.png binary
   *.webp binary
   *.xlsx binary
   *.xls binary
   *.parquet binary
   *.feather binary

   # Large files that should use git-lfs eventually (tracked for future):
   # *.jsonl diff=jsonl   (custom diff driver for bead store)
   ```

3. Optional: `[*.{jsonl}] end_of_line = lf insert_final_newline = false` — bead JSONL expects no-trailing-newline per append-only invariant.

Blast radius: 2 new tiny files at repo root (~20-30 lines total). Zero source changes. Zero test changes.

Prerequisites: None. Coordinate with br-thty (rustfmt.toml) + br-0l5r (cross-platform CI, where line-ending drift would first surface) for maximum impact.

**Related:**
- br-thty (rustfmt.toml) — companion style-enforcement for Rust.
- br-0l5r (cross-platform CI) — Windows matrix cell would hit CRLF issues without this.
- br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — one-liner reference: "Your editor should auto-apply .editorconfig."","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:42:38.537873244Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:34:43.107165666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:34:43.104657741Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-platform","editorconfig","review","style"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-xh0","title":"Add sort_index metamorphic properties","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T11:31:10.314044063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T11:42:43.377626514Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T11:42:43.375866199Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-xha7","title":"[HIGH] bench: no differential benchmark vs pandas — 'Profile-proven performance' + '87% speedup' README claims unbacked by CI","description":"Applying /testing-fuzzing skill's Oracle Hierarchy + /extreme-software-optimization's "performance claims must be continuously verified" principle.

**Observation.** README capability row markets performance AND a specific claim ("87% speedup via memoization and dense aggregation paths" for groupby, plus "Profile-proven performance" overall). The supporting infrastructure:

- crates/fp-conformance/tests/perf_baselines.rs — 387 lines measuring fp-frame latencies ALONE. No pandas comparison. No baseline-over-time storage. #[ignore] by default (br-ing6 covers).
- find . -name benches -type d  → **zero bench directories** in any crate.
- grep for criterion in Cargo.toml files → **zero matches**.
- ci.yml line 46 `cargo bench` — no-op because no [[bench]] blocks exist (br-ing6 covers).

**Gap.** The claim "we're 87% faster than the previous implementation via AG-04/AG-05" is a frankenpandas-vs-frankenpandas claim (our old code vs our new code). Nowhere do we have a frankenpandas-vs-pandas benchmark — the only comparison a user cares about. "Drop-in pandas API in safe Rust" invites a fair comparison: on identical input, how much faster is fp-frame.groupby().sum() than pd.DataFrame.groupby().sum()?

**Skill rules + /extreme-software-optimization hooks:**
- Oracle Hierarchy: pandas is the REFERENCE implementation. Using it as a differential oracle for PERFORMANCE (not just correctness) is the stronger measurement.
- /extreme-software-optimization skill: "performance regressions must fail CI; performance claims must be continuously verified against a reference." We fail both.

**Concrete consequence.** When a user lands on the README and reads "Profile-proven performance", then clones + runs a quick `fp-frame groupby` vs `pd.DataFrame.groupby` timing comparison and finds fp-frame is, say, 1.2x faster (not the implied 10-100x for a "Rust rewrite"), credibility drops. The marketing is under-backed. Conversely: if fp-frame IS 10x faster, not showing that continuously in CI means we have no defense against a future regression.

**Severity.** HIGH — release-time credibility is on the line. README's performance positioning is the primary differentiator from Polars (Polars wins on raw speed but loses on pandas API parity — our play is "speed AND parity"). The speed half is un-gated.

**Fix shape:**

1. New crate directory `crates/fp-conformance/benches/` (or a new crate `fp-benchmarks`):
   - `vs_pandas.rs` — uses criterion. Each bench group:
     ```rust
     fn bench_groupby_sum_1k(c: &mut Criterion) {
         let frame = generate_typical_frame(1_000);
         let pandas_bytes = serialize_as_csv_for_pandas(&frame);

         c.bench_function("fp_frame::groupby_sum/1k", |b| {
             b.iter(|| frame.groupby(&["k"]).unwrap().sum().unwrap())
         });
         c.bench_function("pandas::groupby_sum/1k", |b| {
             b.iter(|| pandas_subprocess_groupby_sum(&pandas_bytes))
         });
     }
     ```
   - The pandas half calls pandas_oracle.py subprocess (or uses the PyO3 bindings once br-diic ships) to measure pandas timing. Subprocess-per-iter has overhead; use criterion's `iter_batched` + pre-loaded subprocess.

2. Bench classes to cover (match the readme claims):
   - DataFrame construction (from_dict, from_records, from_series with alignment)
   - GroupBy: agg, agg_named, rolling.mean, resample.sum
   - Arithmetic: df + df, series + series (with and without alignment)
   - Merge: inner, left, outer at 10k/100k/1M sizes
   - IO: read_csv, read_parquet
   - Reshape: set_index_multi, reset_index, stack, unstack

3. Baseline storage: criterion's `--save-baseline current` + `--baseline previous` produces regression reports. Ship the "previous" baseline as a committed JSON fixture so CI can compare.

4. CI integration: scheduled nightly job runs the bench suite; generates an artifact `artifacts/bench/vs_pandas_report.md`. Posts to a Github Pages dashboard. Fails CI if fp-frame / pandas ratio regresses more than 20% from the committed baseline.

5. README badge: once the bench suite produces reliable numbers, replace "Profile-proven performance" with a concrete "5-20x faster than pandas on typical GroupBy workloads (see benchmarks)" link to the dashboard.

Blast radius: ~200-300 lines across 5-10 bench files, +1 ci.yml scheduled workflow (~40 lines), +1 baseline.json (~10 KB), README update. Requires pandas subprocess interop (or PyO3 once diic lands). Zero source-logic changes in fp-*.

Prerequisites:
- br-d6xa (CI pandas install) — prerequisite for CI bench runs.
- br-ing6 (perf regression gate) — this bead is the pandas-reference version of ing6; ing6 measures us vs us, this measures us vs pandas. Both needed.

**Related:**
- br-k05t (scale fixtures) — scale conformance fixtures naturally become bench fixtures.
- br-liai (differential vs pandas fuzz) — shares oracle infra for "same input, two impls" pattern.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:28.238226469Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:28.238226469Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bench","differential","performance","review"]}

@@ -17,3 +17,23 @@ Conventions:
 CI:
 - `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs the `fuzz-regression` job on every push and pull request
 - `.github/workflows/fuzz-nightly.yml` runs longer nightly campaigns and uploads any new crash artifacts
+- `.github/workflows/fuzz-minimize.yml` runs `cargo fuzz tmin` / `cmin` weekly (Sunday 07:00 UTC) per br-i9rj
+
+Dictionaries (`-dict=` on the libFuzzer command line):
+- `fuzz/dictionaries/csv.dict` — CSV / RFC 4180 delimiters + quoting + BOM
+- `fuzz/dictionaries/json.dict` — JSON literals + escapes + numeric edges + deep-nesting seeds
+- `fuzz/dictionaries/excel.dict` — ZIP magic + xlsx XML fragments + XLS compound-file magic
+- `fuzz/dictionaries/parquet.dict` — PAR1 magic + Thrift type/codec/encoding tokens
+- `fuzz/dictionaries/arrow_ipc.dict` — ARROW1 + record-batch metadata + type tokens
+
+Attach the relevant dictionary to any manual `cargo fuzz run` invocation, e.g.
+`cargo fuzz run fuzz_csv_parse corpus/fuzz_csv_parse -- -dict=dictionaries/csv.dict`.
+Per /testing-fuzzing Rule 4, structure-aware dictionaries typically accelerate coverage
+5-50x on structured-format parsers.
+
+Sanitizers:
+- cargo-fuzz defaults to AddressSanitizer (ASan). Per /testing-fuzzing Rule 5, prefer
+  ASan + UBSan together. Invoke explicitly under nightly:
+  `RUSTFLAGS="-Zsanitizer=address,undefined" cargo +nightly fuzz run <target>`.
+- MSan / TSan are separate campaigns (ASan + MSan are incompatible). Ship-ready crash
+  artifacts from any sanitizer campaign belong in `fuzz/regressions/` (see br-lvl6).

@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@
+# libFuzzer dictionary for Apache Arrow IPC (stream + file) fuzz targets.
+# Per br-frankenpandas-gztr + /testing-fuzzing Rule 4.
+
+# Arrow file format magic (header + footer).
+"ARROW1"
+
+# IPC stream end-of-stream marker (continuation).
+"\xff\xff\xff\xff\x00\x00\x00\x00"
+
+# IPC continuation marker.
+"\xff\xff\xff\xff"
+
+# Record-batch metadata tokens (flatbuffer schemas).
+"Schema"
+"RecordBatch"
+"DictionaryBatch"
+"Message"
+"Field"
+"Tensor"
+"SparseTensor"
+
+# Type tokens.
+"Null"
+"Int"
+"FloatingPoint"
+"Binary"
+"Utf8"
+"LargeUtf8"
+"LargeBinary"
+"Bool"
+"Decimal"
+"Date"
+"Time"
+"Timestamp"
+"Interval"
+"Duration"
+"List"
+"LargeList"
+"FixedSizeList"
+"Struct_"
+"Union"
+"Map"
+
+# Endianness tokens.
+"Little"
+"Big"
+
+# Metadata tokens common to IPC headers.
+"version"
+"endianness"
+"features"
+"custom_metadata"
+"dictionaries"
+"buffers"
+"nodes"
+
+# Common small flatbuffer offsets.
+"\x04\x00"
+"\x08\x00"
+"\x10\x00"
+"\x20\x00"

@@ -0,0 +1,55 @@
+# libFuzzer dictionary for CSV / RFC 4180 fuzz targets.
+# Format: one token per line; quoted strings support \t, \n, \r, \xHH.
+# Per br-frankenpandas-gztr + /testing-fuzzing Rule 4.
+
+# Field / record separators.
+","
+";"
+"\t"
+"|"
+"\n"
+"\r\n"
+"\r"
+
+# Quoting + escaping.
+"\""
+"\"\""
+"\"\n\""
+"\",\""
+"\"\t\""
+
+# Common header tokens.
+"id,"
+"name,"
+"value,"
+"timestamp,"
+"true"
+"false"
+"null"
+"NaN"
+"NA"
+"None"
+
+# Byte-order mark + magic bytes.
+"\xef\xbb\xbf"
+
+# Numeric edges.
+"0"
+"-0"
+"-1"
+"0.0"
+"-0.0"
+"1e308"
+"1e-308"
+"inf"
+"-inf"
+
+# ISO-8601 stubs.
+"2026-01-01"
+"2026-04-22T12:00:00Z"
+"1970-01-01T00:00:00.000Z"
+
+# Problem bytes often found in CSV injection / DoS corpora.
+"=cmd|"
+"\\x00"
+"\\xff"

@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@
+# libFuzzer dictionary for Excel .xlsx / .xls fuzz targets.
+# Per br-frankenpandas-gztr + /testing-fuzzing Rule 4.
+# XLSX is a zip archive; XLS is a compound file binary format.
+
+# ZIP local-file header magic ("PK\x03\x04").
+"\x50\x4b\x03\x04"
+
+# ZIP central-directory header.
+"\x50\x4b\x01\x02"
+
+# ZIP end-of-central-directory record.
+"\x50\x4b\x05\x06"
+
+# ZIP64 extensions.
+"\x50\x4b\x06\x06"
+"\x50\x4b\x06\x07"
+
+# XLS / compound file binary format (CFB) magic.
+"\xd0\xcf\x11\xe0\xa1\xb1\x1a\xe1"
+
+# xlsx sharedStrings.xml / workbook.xml token fragments.
+"<sharedStrings"
+"<si><t>"
+"</t></si>"
+"<sheet "
+"<workbook "
+"<sst "
+"<row "
+"<c "
+" t=\"s\""
+" t=\"n\""
+" t=\"b\""
+" t=\"e\""
+"#REF!"
+"#VALUE!"
+"#NAME?"
+"#DIV/0!"
+
+# Relationship file fragments.
+"<Relationship"
+"xmlns=\"http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/"
+
+# Content-types / app.xml fragments.
+"[Content_Types].xml"
+"_rels/.rels"
+
+# Formula-injection tokens (CVE-2014-3524 et al.).
+"=cmd|"
+"=HYPERLINK("
+"+cmd|"

@@ -0,0 +1,65 @@
+# libFuzzer dictionary for JSON / JSONL fuzz targets.
+# Per br-frankenpandas-gztr + /testing-fuzzing Rule 4.
+
+# Structural tokens.
+"{"
+"}"
+"["
+"]"
+":"
+","
+"\""
+
+# Literals.
+"true"
+"false"
+"null"
+"NaN"
+"Infinity"
+"-Infinity"
+
+# String escape sequences (RFC 7159 §7).
+"\\\""
+"\\\\"
+"\\/"
+"\\b"
+"\\f"
+"\\n"
+"\\r"
+"\\t"
+"\\u0000"
+"\\u0041"
+"\\u00ff"
+"\\uffff"
+"\\uD83D\\uDE00"
+"\\uD800"
+"\\uDFFF"
+
+# Numeric edges.
+"0"
+"-0"
+"0.0"
+"-1.7976931348623157e+308"
+"1.7976931348623157e+308"
+"5e-324"
+"2.2250738585072014e-308"
+
+# Deep-nesting seeds (stack-overflow amplifiers).
+"[[[[[["
+"{\"a\":{\"a\":{\"a\":"
+"]]]]]]"
+"}}}"
+
+# JSON Lines separators.
+"\n"
+"\r\n"
+
+# Common key name tokens.
+"\"id\""
+"\"name\""
+"\"data\""
+"\"value\""
+"\"error\""
+"\"schema\""
+"\"rows\""
+"\"columns\""

@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+# libFuzzer dictionary for Apache Parquet fuzz targets.
+# Per br-frankenpandas-gztr + /testing-fuzzing Rule 4.
+
+# PAR1 file magic (header + footer).
+"PAR1"
+"PARE"
+
+# Thrift compact protocol tokens commonly used in page/column headers.
+"\x15"
+"\x16"
+"\x19"
+"\x1c"
+
+# Physical type tokens.
+"BOOLEAN"
+"INT32"
+"INT64"
+"INT96"
+"FLOAT"
+"DOUBLE"
+"BYTE_ARRAY"
+"FIXED_LEN_BYTE_ARRAY"
+
+# Logical type tokens.
+"STRING"
+"UTF8"
+"DATE"
+"TIME_MILLIS"
+"TIMESTAMP_MILLIS"
+"TIMESTAMP_MICROS"
+"TIMESTAMP_NANOS"
+"DECIMAL"
+"MAP"
+"LIST"
+"ENUM"
+
+# Codec tokens.
+"SNAPPY"
+"GZIP"
+"LZ4"
+"ZSTD"
+"BROTLI"
+"LZO"
+"UNCOMPRESSED"
+
+# Encoding tokens.
+"PLAIN"
+"PLAIN_DICTIONARY"
+"RLE"
+"RLE_DICTIONARY"
+"BIT_PACKED"
+"DELTA_BINARY_PACKED"
+"DELTA_BYTE_ARRAY"
+"BYTE_STREAM_SPLIT"
+
+# Page-type tokens.
+"DATA_PAGE"
+"DATA_PAGE_V2"
+"DICTIONARY_PAGE"
+"INDEX_PAGE"
+
+# Repetition tokens.
+"REQUIRED"
+"OPTIONAL"
+"REPEATED"
+
+# Row-group / column chunk metadata tokens.
+"num_rows"
+"total_byte_size"
+"column_chunks"
